title: Xerisced French Chicken Braised Casserole
description: Charred, springy French dish featuring stoned dates and a rich, low-sodium broth. Inspired by coq-au-vin.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
Season the chicken with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the chicken until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add the chopped onion to the pot.
Cook the onion until caramelized, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the red wine to the pot and cook until reduced by half, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken broth to the pot and bring to a simmer.
Return the chicken to the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside to rest.
Melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the stoned dates to the saucepan and cook until caramelized, about 5 minutes.
Add the all-purpose flour to the saucepan and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Strain the braising liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan.
Bring the braising liquid to a simmer and cook until reduced by half, about 5 minutes.
Add the caramelized dates and their sauce to the saucepan with the braising liquid.
Return the chicken to the pot and toss to coat with the sauce.
Serve the chicken hot, garnished with fresh thyme.
Serve the braising liquid on the side for dipping the chicken.
